home.heroTagline: Front-end architecture and product delivery for Agent, RAG, and Workflow systems
home.aboutMeDesc: 5+ years of experience in front-end engineering, spanning large-scale Web applications, complex interaction design, and front-end architecture. My current focus is AI product development across Agent, RAG, Workflow, and multimodal scenarios, translating model capability into product-grade UX, scalable state systems, and maintainable front-end delivery.
home.ctaDesc: If you want a faster read on my background, projects, and strengths, ask the AI grounded in my resume context.

digitalHuman.voiceUnsupported: Speech recognition not supported in this browser
digitalHuman.comingSoon: 3D avatar and voice chat coming soon

askAi.title: Ask AI about my experience
askAi.subtitle: RAG + personal knowledge base · Answers from resume only
askAi.emptyHint: Click an example below or type your question
askAi.footer: Answers are based on resume only. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.
askAi.example1: What AI projects has Liu Feng done?
askAi.example3: Is he a fit for an AI engineer role?

aiDemo.title: AI Demo
aiDemo.subtitle: Interactive product demos across AI workflows, agents, and knowledge systems
aiDemo.landingIntro: This is not a gallery of isolated features. It is a curated set of AI product case studies that show how I translate capabilities such as agents, knowledge chat, and workflow systems into product-grade interaction patterns, scalable state architecture, and polished front-end delivery.
aiDemo.landingStat1Desc: The focus is not isolated screens, but end-to-end interaction chains, state orchestration, and product behavior.
aiDemo.landingStat2Desc: Start with execution UX, then RAG product design, then visual orchestration for the clearest story.
aiDemo.landingStat3Desc: Able to design complex front-end systems and turn model capability into usable, polished, and extensible product interfaces.
aiDemo.whyTitle: Why this portfolio is worth reviewing
aiDemo.whyDesc: Compared with a typical online resume, this portfolio makes product judgment and engineering capability easier to verify. It shows not only what I can build, but how I structure interaction, state, and delivery around AI-native products.
aiDemo.whyCard1Title: Not a static showcase, but architectural thinking
aiDemo.whyCard1Desc: Each demo maps to a recognizable AI product shape, making it easier to evaluate my judgment in information architecture, state layering, component abstraction, and complex interaction design.
aiDemo.whyCard2Title: Not model wrapping, but complete product UX
aiDemo.whyCard2Desc: The emphasis is not just on calling a model, but on the full experience around it: step flow, cited answers, retrieval explainability, artifact delivery, failure states, and feedback loops.
aiDemo.whyCard3Title: Not one-off demos, but extensible systems
aiDemo.whyCard3Desc: These pages are structured like iterative products, so they can be extended naturally with real model integrations, back-end services, configuration surfaces, auth, and analytics.
aiDemo.landingGuideDesc: Follow this route to understand the main AI product story in this online resume more quickly.
aiDemo.landingGuideStep1Desc: Begin with the timeline, artifacts, streaming steps, and multi-state execution flow to see the strongest agent UX signals first.
aiDemo.landingGuideStep2Desc: Focus on citations, RAG trace, feedback logs, and config switching to see how I approach knowledge product UX and explainability.
aiDemo.landingGuideStep3Desc: Use the visual workflow editor to see node-based orchestration, execution order, and low-code interaction patterns.
aiDemo.reviewerTitle: How different reviewers can navigate it
aiDemo.reviewerDesc: This portfolio includes multiple reading paths so HR, front-end interviewers, and AI interviewers can each reach the most relevant signal quickly.
aiDemo.reviewerHrDesc: A quick way to assess my positioning, seniority, and the overall completeness of the portfolio.
aiDemo.reviewerHrFocus: Focus on whether the site feels productized, whether the demos communicate business value clearly, and whether the portfolio fits an AI-facing front-end role.
aiDemo.reviewerHrPath: Start with Agent, scan Knowledge and Workflow, then open the full resume page to review experience and project depth.
aiDemo.reviewerFrontendDesc: A quick way to evaluate complex interaction design, state organization, component systems, and editor-style UX.
aiDemo.reviewerFrontendFocus: Focus on the timeline, artifact panel, RAG config panel, workflow node interactions, and consistency across responsive details.
aiDemo.reviewerFrontendPath: Start with Workflow for interaction design, then inspect Agent for execution-state UX, and finish with Knowledge for the right-panel information architecture.
aiDemo.reviewerAiTitle: For AI product / AI engineering interviewers
aiDemo.reviewerAiDesc: A quick way to evaluate whether I understand the product shape of agents, RAG, and workflow systems.
aiDemo.reviewerAiFocus: Focus on cited answers, retrieval trace, tool use, step flow, artifact generation, and workflow orchestration patterns.
aiDemo.reviewerAiPath: Start with Agent execution flow, continue with Knowledge explainability, and finish with Workflow orchestration and runtime logic.
aiDemo.landingGridDesc: Each module represents a different AI product capability. These are not isolated experiments, but product-oriented frontend implementations.
aiDemo.cardNextHint: A strong next module to continue exploring
aiDemo.workflowDesc: Langflow / Dify–style visual workflow editor; drag-and-drop nodes to build AI pipelines.
aiDemo.workflowNodes: Nodes: Prompt, LLM, Knowledge, Tool, Output
aiDemo.workflowTech: Node drag-and-drop · DAG · State · Real-time execution · Vue Flow / React Flow
aiDemo.workflowValue: Shows node orchestration, config interaction, execution feedback, and complex state synchronization in AI workflow products.
aiDemo.workflowProof: Demonstrates that I can build more than pages: I can ship editor-like UIs, graph interactions, and orchestration-oriented front-end architecture.
aiDemo.workflowBestFor: Best for visual editor and workflow orchestration capability
aiDemo.agentDesc: Simulates AI Agent task execution (AutoGPT / LangChain–style); Step Flow decomposition.
aiDemo.agentTech: Streaming UI · Task Timeline · Tool call visualization · ReAct Agent UI
aiDemo.agentValue: Highlights timeline UX, tool execution, artifact generation, final answers, and multi-state feedback in agent products.
aiDemo.agentProof: Shows I can translate complex agent execution into clear, product-grade front-end interaction instead of a stitched demo experience.
aiDemo.agentBestFor: Best starting point for execution flow and agent product UX
aiDemo.knowledgeDesc: Enterprise knowledge-base AI chat with citation and semantic search.
aiDemo.knowledgeTech: Streaming Chat · Citation highlight · Source display · Semantic search
aiDemo.knowledgeValue: Covers citations, RAG config, retrieval trace, feedback loops, and document browsing for knowledge-based AI products.
aiDemo.knowledgeProof: Shows I understand that knowledge chat is more than a chat box: it also needs explainability, feedback, config visibility, and retrieval UX.
aiDemo.knowledgeBestFor: Best for RAG productization and explainable AI UX
aiDemo.workflowPageSubtitle: A visual workflow studio for AI orchestration, combining node composition, configuration editing, and runtime result review.
aiDemo.workflowBadge: Visual orchestration + node execution + runtime flow
aiDemo.workflowHeroStat1Value: Build Prompt / LLM / Knowledge / Output orchestration visually
aiDemo.workflowHeroStat2Value: Editor-grade interaction, node config modeling, and execution-linked UI
aiDemo.workflowHeroStat3Value: Best for showing AI orchestration product thinking and complex editor UX
aiDemo.workflowStudioDesc: This page is more than a canvas. It brings node orchestration, node config, runtime input, and result review into one operable AI flow workspace.
aiDemo.workflowGuide1: 1. Drag or add nodes on the canvas to build a Prompt, Knowledge, LLM, and Output execution chain.
aiDemo.workflowGuide2: 2. Select a node to edit its content and system prompt so each node can carry a different role.
aiDemo.workflowGuide3: 3. Run a real task below and the output will follow the current graph structure instead of a fixed template.
aiDemo.workflowRunEmptyHint: Enter input and click Run to execute the flow (e.g. Prompt → LLM → Knowledge → Output)
aiDemo.workflowNodeContentPrompt: Prompt text; if empty, use upstream input
aiDemo.workflowNodeContentLLM: Default user message (optional); if empty, use upstream
aiDemo.workflowNodeContentKnowledge: Custom knowledge; if empty, use resume context
aiDemo.workflowNodeContentTool: Tool description; if empty, show "Tool executed"
aiDemo.agentPageSubtitle: A product-oriented agent execution demo showing how task decomposition, tool use, artifact generation, and final response composition can be expressed in the UI.
aiDemo.agentBadge: Step Flow + tool use + artifact generation
aiDemo.agentHeroStat1Value: Task decomposition, staged streaming, and execution-timeline visibility
aiDemo.agentHeroStat2Value: Front-end storytelling, state design, and artifact delivery for complex AI execution
aiDemo.agentHeroStat3Value: Best for showing agent productization, execution visibility, and interaction quality
aiDemo.agentTaskDesc: Start a full agent run from a preset or custom task to show the task entry and operator experience quickly.
aiDemo.agentTimelineDesc: Shows analysis, tool calls, observations, and artifact generation step by step so the execution stays visible and reviewable.
aiDemo.agentArtifactDesc: Inspect HTML, Markdown, and JSON artifacts in one place with preview, source, metadata, and diff views.
aiDemo.agentFinalDesc: Summarizes the current run in a format that feels closer to a real product delivery than a raw demo response.
aiDemo.agentTaskPlaceholder: Enter a task, e.g. generate a SaaS login prototype or write an AI workflow feature brief or content outline
aiDemo.agentFinalEmpty: Run the agent to see the final answer here.
aiDemo.agentTimelineEmpty: After you run a task, the timeline will show task analysis, tool calls, and result synthesis.
aiDemo.agentArtifactEmpty: When the task generates a page prototype or Markdown artifact, the preview will appear here.
aiDemo.agentMetadataJsonKeysEmpty: No JSON keys could be extracted from this artifact.
aiDemo.agentDiffEmpty: Generate the same artifact type at least twice to see version differences here.
aiDemo.agentDiffNoChanges: No content differences were detected between the latest two versions.
aiDemo.agentSuggestion1: Generate a modern login page prototype with a blue-purple gradient theme
aiDemo.agentSuggestion2: Design an agent console homepage with a task area, timeline, and artifact panel
aiDemo.agentSuggestion3: Write a Markdown feature brief and content outline about AI Workflow Builder
aiDemo.knowledgePageSubtitle: A RAG-oriented knowledge demo for enterprise scenarios, emphasizing streaming answers, citations, retrieval explainability, and source-level traceability.
aiDemo.knowledgeHeroStat1Value: Cited answers, matched sources, document detail, and multi-turn retrieval visibility
aiDemo.knowledgeHeroStat2Value: RAG config visibility, explainability UX, and answer-quality feedback loops
aiDemo.knowledgeHeroStat3Value: Best for showing knowledge-product UX, retrieval visibility, and operational readiness
aiDemo.knowledgeChatDesc: Connects questioning, retrieval, citations, feedback, and chat UX into one complete knowledge-answering entry point.
aiDemo.knowledgeSourcesDesc: Turns sources into an operable workspace with matched results, filters, and a document library entry.
aiDemo.knowledgeTraceDesc: Visualizes rewrite, recall, rerank, and answer generation to make retrieval logic explainable and tunable.
aiDemo.knowledgeFeedbackDesc: Accumulates user feedback into trends and samples to show product operations awareness and answer-quality analysis.
aiDemo.knowledgeComparisonDesc: Compares recent retrieval runs and configs in a more productized way, so tuning differences are easier to see.
aiDemo.knowledgeSourceDetailDesc: Inspect source detail, matched snippets, and full content, and jump from citations to the exact source.
aiDemo.knowledgeStreaming: Generating an answer from retrieved sources…
aiDemo.knowledgeEmptyTitle: Ask anything that is covered by the knowledge base
aiDemo.knowledgeEmptyDesc: This demo retrieves relevant source snippets first, then streams a grounded answer with citations.
aiDemo.knowledgeExample1: Why should a knowledge chat UI show source citations?
aiDemo.knowledgeExample2: How should chunking and metadata be designed during enterprise document ingestion?
aiDemo.knowledgeExample3: What should retrieval and reranking pay attention to in multi-turn chat?
aiDemo.knowledgeInputPlaceholder: Ask a question, e.g. How can a knowledge chat reduce hallucinations and improve explainability?
aiDemo.knowledgeHelper: Press Enter to send and Shift + Enter for a new line. Answers will try to include citations such as [S1] and [S2].
aiDemo.knowledgeSourcesEmpty: Once you send a question, the matched sources, scores, and summaries will appear here.
aiDemo.knowledgeSourceDetailEmpty: Select a source to inspect its summary, matched snippets, and full content.
aiDemo.knowledgeFilteredEmpty: No sources match the current filters. Adjust the category, tag, or keyword.
aiDemo.knowledgeCitationJumpHint: Click [S1] / [S2] in the answer to focus the matching source
aiDemo.knowledgeLibrarySearchPlaceholder: Search titles, summaries, content, or tags…
aiDemo.knowledgeLibraryEmpty: No documents match the current filters.
aiDemo.knowledgeTraceEmpty: After you run a question, this panel will show the rewritten query, extracted keywords, retrieved count, and grounding strategy.
aiDemo.knowledgeTraceKeywordsEmpty: No clear keywords were extracted for this turn.
aiDemo.knowledgeFeedbackEmpty: Once you rate any answer, this panel will show local feedback logs and stats.
aiDemo.knowledgeConfigDesc: Adjust the current retrieval and answer chain visually to demo different RAG behaviors.
aiDemo.knowledgeComparisonEmpty: After at least two runs, this panel will compare the latest two configs and retrieval results.

aiProjects.title: AI Projects
aiProjects.subtitle: Selected project work across AI platforms, agents, knowledge systems, workflow products, and multimodal scenarios, with emphasis on product delivery and frontend architecture.
